• Main Page
  • Modules
  • Data Structures
  • Files
  • Examples

USB Device API (gxio/usb_dev.h)
[gxio Low-Level IO Device Control API]

Detailed Description

The USB shim allows access to the processor's Universal Serial Bus connections.

In order to use the USB device shim, it must be enabled in the hypervisor configuration file; the default .hvc will do this (and disable the port 0 USB host shim) if --hvd USB_DEV is added to tile-monitor's command line arguments.

If the system is configured to enable the USB device shim in boot/debug mode (which enables the use of tile-monitor and tile-btk over USB), then that functionality will persist until gxio_usb_dev_init() is called. At that point, the application has control of the shim, and boot/debug operations will no longer work. In some cases, the boot/debug feature will be reenabled once gxio_usb_dev_destroy() is called, but this is not a supported feature of the hardware and should thus not be relied upon.

Data Structures

struct  gxio_usb_dev_context_t
 A context object used to manage USB hardware resources. More...

Functions

int gxio_usb_dev_init (gxio_usb_dev_context_t *context, int usb_index)
 Initialize a USB device context.
int gxio_usb_dev_destroy (gxio_usb_dev_context_t *context)
 Destroy a USB device context.
void gxio_usb_dev_read_rx_fifo (gxio_usb_dev_context_t *context, int ep, void *dest, int len)
 Read data from an endpoint's receive FIFO.
void gxio_usb_dev_write_tx_fifo (gxio_usb_dev_context_t *context, int ep, void *src, int len)
 Write data to an endpoint's transmit FIFO.
unsigned int gxio_usb_dev_get_in_ctrl (gxio_usb_dev_context_t *context, int ep)
 Get the value of an endpoint's input control register.
void gxio_usb_dev_set_in_ctrl (gxio_usb_dev_context_t *context, int ep, unsigned int val)
 Set the value of an endpoint's input control register.
unsigned int gxio_usb_dev_get_in_sts (gxio_usb_dev_context_t *context, int ep)
 Get the value of an endpoint's input status register.
void gxio_usb_dev_set_in_sts (gxio_usb_dev_context_t *context, int ep, unsigned int val)
 Set the value of an endpoint's input status register.
unsigned int gxio_usb_dev_get_in_bufsize (gxio_usb_dev_context_t *context, int ep)
 Get the value of an endpoint's input buffer size register.
void gxio_usb_dev_set_in_bufsize (gxio_usb_dev_context_t *context, int ep, unsigned int val)
 Set the value of an endpoint's input buffer size register.
unsigned int gxio_usb_dev_get_in_mpkt_sz (gxio_usb_dev_context_t *context, int ep)
 Get the value of an endpoint's input maximum packet size register.
void gxio_usb_dev_set_in_mpkt_sz (gxio_usb_dev_context_t *context, int ep, unsigned int val)
 Set the value of an endpoint's input maximum packet size register.

 Get the value of an endpoint's output control register.

 Set the value of an endpoint's output control register.

 Get the value of an endpoint's output_status register.
void gxio_usb_dev_set_out_sts (gxio_usb_dev_context_t *context, int ep, unsigned int val)

 Get the value of an endpoint's output buffer size register.

 Set the value of the endpoint interrupt mask register.

 Get the value of an physical endpoint's configuration register.
void gxio_usb_dev_set_udc_ep (gxio_usb_dev_context_t *context, int ep, unsigned int val)
 Set the value of an physical endpoint's configuration register.
unsigned int gxio_usb_dev_get_udc_vcon (gxio_usb_dev_context_t *context)
 Get the value of the ULPI PHY access register.

 Set the value of the ULPI PHY access register.
int gxio_usb_dev_cfg_interrupt (gxio_usb_dev_context_t *context, int target_cpu, int target_event)
 Request USB device interrupts.
int gxio_usb_dev_reset_interrupt (gxio_usb_dev_context_t *context)
 Reset the USB device interrupt, allowing subsequent device interrupts to be delivered.

Function Documentation

int gxio_usb_dev_init ( gxio_usb_dev_context_t *  context,
int  usb_index 
)

Initialize a USB device context.

A properly initialized context must be obtained before any of the other gxio_usb_dev routines may be used.

Note: once this call returns, the application must finish initializing the physical endpoint registers (via gxio_usb_dev_set_udc_ep()) within 6 milliseconds; otherwise, the system may hang.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a gxio_usb_dev_context_t, which will be initialized by this routine, if it succeeds.
usb_index Index of the USB shim to use.
Returns:
Zero if the context was successfully initialized, else a GXIO_ERR_xxx error code.
Examples:
usb_dev/xfer/ud.c.

int gxio_usb_dev_destroy ( gxio_usb_dev_context_t *  context  ) 

Destroy a USB device context.

Once destroyed, a context may not be used with any gxio_usb_dev routines other than gxio_usb_dev_init(). After this routine returns, no further interrupts requested on this context will be delivered.

Parameters:
context Pointer to an initialized gxio_usb_dev_context_t.
Returns:
Zero if the context was successfully destroyed, else a GXIO_ERR_xxx error code.
Examples:
usb_dev/xfer/ud.c.

void gxio_usb_dev_read_rx_fifo ( gxio_usb_dev_context_t *  context,
int  ep,
void *  dest,
int  len 
)

Read data from an endpoint's receive FIFO.

Note that the hardware does not permit partial reads; when notified that an X-byte packet is available, you must retrieve all X bytes with one call to this routine. Also note that it is legal to call this routine with a len of zero; this is necessary in order to remove a zero-length packet from the FIFO.

Parameters:
context Pointer to an initialized gxio_usb_dev_context_t.
ep Logical endpoint number.
dest Pointer to a memory buffer which will receive the read data.
len Number of bytes of data to read.
Examples:
usb_dev/xfer/ud.c.

void gxio_usb_dev_write_tx_fifo ( gxio_usb_dev_context_t *  context,
int  ep,
void *  src,
int  len 
)

Write data to an endpoint's transmit FIFO.

Note that the hardware does not permit partial writes; to send an X-byte packet, you must write all of its data with one call to this routine. Also note that it is legal to send a null-packet with this routine, by specifying a len of 0 bytes.

Parameters:
context Pointer to an initialized gxio_usb_dev_context_t.
ep Logical endpoint number.
src Pointer to a memory buffer which will supply the write data.
len Number of bytes of data to write.
Examples:
usb_dev/xfer/ud.c.

int  ep 
)

unsigned int  val 
)

Set the value of the ULPI PHY access register.

Parameters:
context Pointer to an initialized gxio_usb_dev_context_t.
val New register value.

int gxio_usb_dev_cfg_interrupt ( gxio_usb_dev_context_t *  context,
int  target_cpu,
int  target_event 
)

Request USB device interrupts.

Request that an IPI interrupt be delivered to a tile when an unmasked USB device interrupt is asserted. Once an interrupt has been delivered, no further interrupts will be delivered until gxio_usb_dev_reset_interrupt() is called.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_usb_dev_context_t.
target_cpu CPU number of interrupt target tile.
target_event Specific event which will be set in the IPI0 register when the interrupt occurs. If this event is -1, then the interrupt will be disabled.
Returns:
Zero if the device was successfully configured to interrupt; otherwise, a nonzero error code.
Examples:
usb_dev/xfer/ud.c.

int gxio_usb_dev_reset_interrupt ( gxio_usb_dev_context_t *  context  ) 

Reset the USB device interrupt, allowing subsequent device interrupts to be delivered.

To prevent lost interrupts, the caller should check for and process device and endpoint events after calling this routine and before returning from the interrupt handler.

Parameters:
context Pointer to a properly initialized gxio_usb_dev_context_t.
Returns:
Zero if the interruptdevice was successfully reset, otherwise, a nonzero error code.
Examples:
usb_dev/xfer/ud.c.
